title: Scope A T1: untrack event ledger artifacts via .gitignore + git rm --cached
description: Make .fabric/events.jsonl, .fabric/events.archive/, and .fabric/events.jsonl.corrupted.* per-machine artifacts by adding them to .gitignore and untracking currently-tracked instances via `git rm --cached`.

files.change: Append three lines under existing .fabric/ section: `.fabric/events.jsonl`, `.fabric/events.archive/`, `.fabric/events.jsonl.corrupted.*`
Run `git rm --cached .fabric/events.jsonl` (file stays on disk, removed from git tracking only)

implementation: Run `git ls-files | grep -E '(events\.jsonl|events\.archive)'` to enumerate currently-tracked instances
Append three gitignore lines as specified
Run `git rm --cached <path>` for each tracked match (keeps working copy)
Verify: `git status` shows deletions staged + untracked file no longer pending modification
Run `pnpm test` (if scoped) or at least `pnpm -r typecheck` to confirm no test references the tracked path

reference.examples: Mirror format of existing `.fabric/.cache/` and `.fabric-personal-dogfood-tmp/` entries

criteria: `git ls-files | grep -E '(events\.jsonl|events\.archive|events\.jsonl\.corrupted)'` returns ZERO lines after staging
`.gitignore` contains exactly three new lines for ledger artifacts
`git status` shows no `M .fabric/events.jsonl` entry after untracking (modification to ignored file is invisible)
Repo working tree still has the on-disk events.jsonl file (only the index entry removed)

rationale.chosen_approach: Plain .gitignore + git rm --cached — minimal infra change
rationale.tradeoffs: History still contains the old events.jsonl revisions (acceptable; trace data not sensitive)

manual_checks: Run `fab doctor` after change — must still detect/handle local events.jsonl
Confirm `cat .fabric/events.jsonl` still readable on the dev machine
success_metrics: Working tree events.jsonl still present and append-writable
